commit.message:
Replace inline Python with probe_command_output module and script

- Add roles/run_benchmarks/library/probe_command_output.py: custom Ansible module
  that runs one or more commands and extracts data via regex or raw output; replaces
  all inline 'python3 -c ...' patterns in shell/command tasks for ffmpeg and crypto
- Add roles/run_benchmarks/files/gen_sortdata.py: extracted sortdata generation
  script (500 k random alphanumeric lines, seed=42) for coreutils benchmark
- roles/run_benchmarks/tasks/ffmpeg.yml: replace inline Python codec discovery with
  probe_command_output; add ansible.builtin.copy for the resulting JSON file
- roles/run_benchmarks/tasks/crypto.yml: replace inline Python algorithm probe with
  probe_command_output; update set_fact to use .data instead of .stdout | from_json
- roles/run_benchmarks/tasks/opencv.yml: replace shell one-liner with
  community.general.python_requirements_info; emulate rc field for downstream tasks
- roles/run_benchmarks/tasks/coreutils.yml: replace inline Python data gen with
  ansible.builtin.script + separate findtree creation task

commit.message:
fix: embed character sprites directly in webview bundle

Bypasses the entire extension host -> postMessage -> Image load
pipeline that has been unreliable. Character PNGs are now base64-
encoded as TypeScript string literals and loaded synchronously at
module init time, guaranteeing they are available before the first
render frame.

The postMessage path (loadCharacterSheetsFromUris) still works as
an override if the extension sends updated characters.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: wire upstream and watch/triage commands in cli-entry.ts (#280)

* fix: wire upstream and watch/triage commands in cli-entry.ts

Both commands had full implementations (upstream.ts, watch.ts) but were
not routed in cli-entry.ts — the recurring 'unwired command' bug class
(issues #224, #236, #237).

- upstream: add import + routing block for upstreamCommand()
- triage/watch: replace placeholder stub with actual runWatch() call
- help text: add upstream command description
- test: clear KNOWN_UNWIRED set (all commands now wired)

Root cause: Brady's cli-entry.ts rewrite (v0.8.21-0.8.23) overwrote
the wiring added in PRs #225 and #238.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: make tests resilient to team rebirths (name-agnostic assertions)

Tests that read live .squad/team.md and .squad/routing.md were hardcoding
agent names (Fenster, Keaton, Hockney). Every time Brady renames the squad
(e.g., Usual Suspects → Apollo 13), these tests break.

Fixed by making live-file tests assert structure and behavior, not names:
- parseRoster(): verify properties exist, not specific member names
- parseRoutingRules(): verify structure, emoji presence, not agent strings
- parseModuleOwnership(): verify shape, not which agent owns which module
- triageIssue(): verify source/confidence, not which agent was selected

Tests with inline fixtures (hardcoded test data) still assert specific
names — those are self-contained and don't break on rebirths.

Also bumps speed-gates line count budget for upstream help text.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix: set __worker_input on globalThis not pyodide.globals

Pyodide's 'from js import X' reads from JavaScript's globalThis (= self in
a Worker), not from pyodide.globals which is Python's namespace.
Using pyodide.globals.set() put the function where Python couldn't find it
via 'from js import', hence the ImportError.

Fix: self.__worker_input = ... (sets on Worker's globalThis)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add per-identity API rate limiting and tighten registration limits (#1264)

Add ApiRateLimiter with sliding-window per-IP tracking for /api/* routes:
- GET (read) operations: 300 requests/minute per identity
- POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE (write) operations: 60 requests/minute per identity
- Lock-free hot path using Interlocked CAS operations
- Automatic stale bucket pruning every 2 minutes

Rate limit check runs centrally in RequestHandler after session context
is built but before route dispatch, returning 429 with Retry-After
header and proper ApiErrorWriter JSON for AJAX callers.

Also:
- Tighten registration per-IP limit from 10 to 3 attempts per window
- Add RequestsInFlight gauge to Prometheus /metrics/prometheus endpoint
- Fix upstream StubMetricsTracker (add EnterRequest/LeaveRequest +
  RequestsInFlight parameter)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
perf: fix ARM64 router regression — branchless AsciiLower, batched metrics, vectorised match

Three hot-path fixes for NativeAOT on ARM64:

1. RecordDispatch: replace 3x Interlocked per call with thread-local
   accumulators flushed every 64 calls. ARM LL/SC (LDAXR/STLXR) is
   2-3x costlier than x86 LOCK prefix; batching amortises by 64x.

2. AsciiLower: replace dual-branch ternary (b >= 'A' && b <= 'Z')
   with SUB/SUB/ASR/AND/ORR arithmetic mask. NativeAOT's ARM64 backend
   emits real conditional branches for ternaries (no JIT to lower to
   CSEL), making the old code unpredictable for mixed-case slugs.

3. TryResolve: pre-lowercase slug once via AsciiLowerSpan, then use
   SequenceEqual for all bucket comparisons. The BCL SequenceEqual
   compiles to AdvSimd-accelerated memcmp under NativeAOT on ARM64.
   Also moves Stopwatch.GetTimestamp() after the empty-slug guard to
   skip the CNTVCT_EL0 system-register read on the fast path.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: setup modal only shows to OWNER on first visit; fix missing setupCompletedAt in GET response

Two root causes:
1. GET /api/tenant/[orgId] never returned setupCompletedAt, so the modal always
   showed (data?.setupCompletedAt was always undefined = falsy = done:false)
2. Modal showed to ALL members but PATCH /api/tenant/[orgId]/setup requires OWNER,
   so non-owners would see the modal and get 403 on submit

Fixes:
- GET /api/tenant/[orgId] now returns setupCompletedAt and userRole fields
  (system admins get synthetic OWNER role, others get their membership role)
- TenantLayout tracks isOwner from the API response; modal only renders when
  setupCompletedAt is null AND userRole === 'OWNER'
- Non-owners and non-system-admins never see the modal

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: PII redaction in log output (#1272)

- Add LogRedactor with span-based char scanning (no regex on hot path)
- Field-level masking: IPs (first octet only), emails (2-char local + domain)
- Free-text email scanner: single-pass O(n), zero alloc when no '@' found
- DiskBufferedLogger.RedactPII flag applied in FormatJsonEntry on structured fields
- Enabled by default via Logging.RedactPII config key (opt-out with false)
- Fix 4 SSO log statements that explicitly embedded email addresses
- Config validation for Logging.RedactPII boolean

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Duplicated lines: 877 → 832 (45 lines removed)
- Clone pairs: 46 → 44 (2 pairs removed)
- Major complexity improvements across affected functions

Completes Step 1 of code quality enhancement plan (PLAN.md).

commit.message:
Add hypervisor health check before each benchmark run

Collect load average, memory, swap, and process count from the
hypervisor before starting benchmarks on each VM. Abort the play
if the hypervisor is overloaded (load/CPU > 2.0, free memory
< 1024 MB, or swap > 90% used with low memory). Thresholds are
configurable via extra vars. The swap check is only enforced when
available memory is also low, avoiding false positives from stale
cold pages in swap.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(hooks): skip heavy checks for tag-only pushes

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix 3 template bugs found during dev/build

- Fix Jinja2 Undefined 'is not none' for missing min/max fields:
  fragment: field.min is not none -> field.get('min') is not none
  (Jinja2 Undefined is not none = True, but dict.get() returns None)
- Filter hidden fields in mui_imports() so unused datetime imports
  are not emitted when only hidden fields have type datetime
- Remove unused 'import React' from Item.test.tsx.j2 (JSX transform)
- Pin vite to ^4.5.0 / @vitejs/plugin-react to ^3.1.0 to avoid
  rollup 4 native .node binary segfault on older Linux systems

Results: tsc --noEmit 0 errors, 4/4 frontend tests pass,
10/10 backend tests pass, npm run dev starts cleanly

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

pings the /api/health endpoint every 15 minutes to keep the Azure

commit.message:
Fix block ID insertion position: use char count not byte len

The rope insert position was computed from String::len() (byte
length) instead of char count, causing IDs to be inserted at
the wrong position — typically the start of the next line
instead of the end of the current line.

Fix: walk backwards through the line's chars to find the content
end, counting in char units that match the rope's coordinate
system.

Add test: test_save_block_ids_multiline verifies correct placement
across paragraph boundaries with newlines.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Refactor buildBatchBuffer to reduce complexity

Extract helper functions from buildBatchBuffer to improve readability:
- encodeStateBaseAddress: Configure GPU base pointers
- encodeScissorCommands: Handle scissor rectangle clipping
- encodeVertexBuffers: Configure vertex buffers with relocations
- encodeVertexElements: Define vertex attribute layout
- encodePrimitive: Emit 3DPRIMITIVE draw calls

Result: Reduced buildBatchBuffer from 78 lines to 31 lines (60% reduction)
Target: <40 lines ✅
Tests: All passing
Complexity: No regressions

Addresses AUDIT.md LOW priority finding.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add bulk block ID assignment after first index

assign_block_ids_bulk() runs once after the first IndexComplete
where files_changed > 0. It reads all .md files from disk, parses
them, generates IDs for blocks without them, and writes back via
the DiskWriter channel. Files with all IDs present are skipped.

Shows notification: 'Assigned block IDs to N files'.

The block_ids_bulk_done flag ensures this runs only once per
session — subsequent index completions (incremental) skip it.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: handle missing introspection-engine and migration-engine packages

In Prisma 5+, both introspection-engine and migration-engine were merged
into schema-engine-cli. Use 'cargo pkgid' for reliable package existence
checks and fall back to copying the schema-engine binary when standalone
packages are absent.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
v0.4: World readability & interaction pass

- Visual interpolation: smooth 60fps rendering over 20 TPS simulation
- Run/sneak movement: Shift=run (1.6x speed), Ctrl=sneak (0.45x)
- Noise/stealth foundation: per-entity noise, AI noise awareness
- Attack arc visualization: melee skills show hit area during frames
- Floating damage numbers: color-coded, fade-out on hit
- Door frame tiles: guaranteed-passable building entrances
- Exit markers: pulsing golden overlay at map exits
- Map transitions: widened exit zones, fade flash, map name display
- Wall rendering: 4-neighbor edge system with cleaner outlines
- Debug overlay: movement state, noise level, controls hint
- Stamina drain while running, sneak dimming effect
- Updated all JSON maps, tiles, zones for new features
- Docs updated for v0.4

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: filter useless timeline detail values (## headings, Pending, empty)

Old PR documents have Diagnosis fields with values like '## Failure
Summary' which render as noise in the timeline. Now parseTimelineDetails
skips key-value pairs where the value starts with ##, is empty, or
is just 'Pending'. Returns empty string if no meaningful content
remains.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
